title: Protocol Authority Consolidation
purpose: Eliminate split authority between project-governance/protocols/ (markdown) and meta/governance/protocols/ (json). Establish meta/governance/protocols/ as the single canonical source.
architectural_constraints: Markdown protocols are reflections, not canonical
All protocol mutations go through JSON canonical layer
Generated reflections must be reproducible deterministically
Archive, do not delete, until reflections are verified

deliverables.description: Archive or convert to generated-only reflections

acceptance_criteria: project-governance/protocols/ contains only generated reflections or is removed
All 10 JSON protocols in meta/governance/protocols/ pass schema validation
deployment-readiness.json conforms to protocol-definition.schema.json or has dedicated schema
Duplicate filename pairs eliminated (single naming convention)
validate-reflections.ts confirms markdown matches canonical JSON hashes
No orphaned reflections (markdown without canonical JSON source)

traceability.description: Consolidated protocol authority: all 10 JSON protocols pass schema validation. Archived 17 old markdown files. Created reflection integrity validator.

failure_modes.scenario: Markdown protocols still referenced by external systems
failure_modes.mitigation: Search all references before archival; maintain redirects during transition
